I haven't yet left Spotify, but I might be getting there sometime in the near future. I have a decade's worth of over 100 carefully curated playlists, including one that is a timeline of my life in music form; all music I've ever listened to put in the exact order it came to me throughout my life. I know it sounds silly, but it means alot to me to see the dates I added them to the playlist on the side; seeing 'added dec 2014' feels monumental to me. 
All that being said, alot of artists I love have left the platform, most recently the black metal band Panopticon, and if more continue to do so it might push me away officially.
I still get emails from Spotify asking me to rethink coming back

the one thing with spotify, is that 1. they allow artist with major label deal get an automatic surfacing, curated playlist, editorial listing. i have advisor confess this in my conversations. 2. they also do stream masking i have proof of this when i upload a track and it took off on youtube getting 150+ views with a retention rate very high, under 2 days, but spotify was showing zero across the board. when i show this within next day the stats showed like 60+ listeners or streams, but all from netherlands, i.e. masking streams.

i left spotify for yt music, but maan i miss spotify. so here's the things i miss:
being able to controll my playback from any device (like im listening to a song on my pc while playing a game, and if i dont like a song i just skip it from my phone or choose a different one, and it will play it on my pc)
searching for something inside of any playlist, and then it will only play those results, for example: i search for the weeknd inside my liked songs, and from those 3000 songs it will only play those that have the weeknd in them.
it remembering whether im shuffleing my song or just one after another. like anytime i press a new song yt forgets that i was shuffleing them and starts playing my songs in normal order again. and then sometimes while listening on shuffle, it also forgets that im shuffleing, so it displays it as not shiuffleing, but it is actually in shuffle, it just doesnt show it, and then i press the shuffle button, and it reshuffles it (its a bug)
AND PLAY NEXT! PLAY NEXT ADDS THE SONG TO THE BEGINNING OF THE QUEUE, WHILE SPOTIFY'S ADD TO QUEUE ADDS IT TO THE END OF THE QUEUE. like that is so much better on spotify. it creates a secondary queue that you add song to to the end of that, and when its over, it continues your old queue, and it also does that while remembering it even if you switch shuffle and not shuffle. that is irreplaceable. yt just clears the queue and you have to add your songs back.
so i only use yt music because the premium also goes over to youtube, and i dont want to pay 2 subcsriptions. but yt is way worse.

You missed the biggest issue with Spotify bro..the playlist. Say you have a playlist that’s years old, has 700+ songs on it, have you ever noticed shuffling the playlist only plays the same damn songs somehow? The same like 100 or so songs? Yeah because that’s what they do. They only play up to like 150 songs out of the entire playlist and it’s based on overall listen amounts, how consistently a song is chosen to play, and a few other rather irrelevant when shuffling stats. If I’m paying monthly for a service and am led to believe I’m getting access to use everything fully as the app is implying then one would think “shuffle” means every song on the playlist has equals chances to be the next song played…not the case. “Shuffle” to them means they’ll select 150 of your not favorite songs based off unknown criteria and that’s your only option unless you wanna use their “special shuffle” where they add a few suggested by them songs to your preselected 150 songs.

I was subbed to Spotify, then I realized that Youtube music also has the same songs that I was looking for, AND I can still put "bootlegs" into a playlist, besides getting the obvious perks for watching videos on youtube. So, I just moved to Youtube premium.

I used to use Spotify because it was a good way to find underground artists for genres like folk metal and power metal. I would start a song radio from a song I liked, and that would lead me to other underground or not well-known artists/songs, which is why my playlist was big. Now, if I click "start a song radio," it doesn't optimize to the song being played and instead adds other genres or artists that I am not in the mood to listen to. For example, if I'm listening to Iron Maiden, I don't want Cannibal Corpse. It doesn't fit the vibe... I've switched to Deezer and enjoyed the experience and better audio
